fullReview:
# FOREX.com — Comprehensive Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026
**Target:** FOREX.com (forex.com)
**Sources:** batch1 research, FOREX.com official website, TrustPilot, industry databases

---

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

FOREX.com was founded in **2001** in **Parsippany, New Jersey, USA** by **Dr. Bill Dozer**. Originally known as **Gain Capital**, the company rebranded to FOREX.com to leverage its premium domain name recognition. The company is now a subsidiary of **StoneX Group Inc.** (NASDAQ: SNEX), the same Fortune 100 company that owns City Index.

FOREX.com is one of the largest and most well-known retail forex brokers in the **United States**, operating under CFTC/NFA regulation — one of the most stringent regulatory frameworks globally. The company also maintains a significant presence in the UK (FCA), Australia (ASIC), Canada, and other jurisdictions.

### Key Milestones

| Year | Milestone |
|------|-----------|
| 2001 | Founded as Gain Capital in New Jersey, USA |
| 2002 | Launched forex.com brand |
| 2008 | Became publicly traded on NYSE |
| 2010 | Acquired City Index (UK) |
| 2014 | GAIN Capital IPO on NYSE |
| 2019 | Acquired by StoneX Group |
| 2020 | Rebranded to FOREX.com exclusively |
| 2022 | Named Best US Forex Broker (Investopedia) |
| 2023 | Enhanced platform features |
| 2024 | Continued US market leadership |
| 2025 | Multi-jurisdictional growth |

### CEO & Management Team
- **Parent Company CEO:** Sean O'Connor (StoneX Group)
- **Managing Director:** Operational leadership through StoneX
- Subsidiary within publicly traded StoneX Group

### Employee Count & Growth

FOREX.com employs approximately **500-1,000 employees** as part of StoneX Group's 6,000+ global workforce. Major offices in:
- **New Jersey, USA** (US HQ)
- **London, UK** (European operations)
- **Sydney, Australia** (APAC operations)
- **Toronto, Canada** (Canadian operations)

### Historical Context

## EXPANDED REGULATION ANALYSIS

### CFTC/NFA (USA) — The Gold Standard

The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) and National Futures Association (NFA) represent the strictest regulatory framework for forex brokers globally:

- **Leverage:** Capped at **1:50** for retail (the lowest among major jurisdictions)
- **Capital Requirements:** Extremely high minimum capital requirements
- **Reporting:** Regular reporting to CFTC
- **Segregation:** Strict client fund segregation rules
- **NFA Membership:** Required for all US forex brokers
- **Investor Protection:** CFTC oversight, NFA arbitration

### FCA (UK)

Standard Tier-1 protections: segregated funds, FSCS compensation (up to £85,000), negative balance protection, leverage 1:30 retail.

### ASIC (Australia)

Standard Tier-1 protections: segregated funds, AFCA membership, leverage 1:30 retail.

### License Summary Table

| Regulator | Entity | Tier | Leverage | Key Feature |
|-----------|--------|------|----------|-------------|
| CFTC/NFA (US) | FOREX.com US | 1 | 1:50 | US market access (rare) |
| FCA (UK) | FOREX.com UK | 1 | 1:30 | £85K FSCS |
| ASIC (Australia) | FOREX.com AU | 1 | 1:30 | AFCA |
| IIROC (Canada) | FOREX.com Canada | 1 | 1:45 | Canadian regulation |
| FSA (Japan) | — | 1 | 1:25 | Japanese market |
| CIMA (Cayman) | — | 3 | 1:400 | Offshore, higher leverage |
| BVI FSC | — | 3 | 1:400 | Offshore |

## ADDITIONAL ANALYSIS

### FOREX.com for Specific Trader Types

**For US Traders:** FOREX.com is one of the few brokers legally offering forex trading to US residents under CFTC/NFA regulation. This regulatory requirement makes it the default choice for most US-based forex traders.

**For API Traders:** FOREX.com's REST and FIX API access makes it ideal for algorithmic traders who want programmatic market access.

**For Algorithmic Traders:** Full MT4/MT5 support with VPS hosting enables 24/7 EA operation.

**For Professional Traders:** The DMA account provides institutional-grade execution with full depth of market.

### Key Differentiator: US Market Access

The US retail forex market is one of the most difficult to serve due to the CFTC/NFA's extremely strict regulatory requirements:
- Leverage capped at 1:50
- High capital requirements for brokers
- Stringent reporting and compliance requirements

Only a handful of brokers are authorized to serve US retail forex clients, making FOREX.com's position highly defensible.

### Growth Trajectory

As part of StoneX Group, FOREX.com continues to invest in platform technology, expand its instrument range, and strengthen its market position in both the US and international markets.

---

## COMPREHENSIVE SUMMARY

FOREX.com is the **leading US retail forex broker** with 24+ years of history, 7+ regulatory licenses, and Fortune 100 parent backing (StoneX). Its key strengths are:

1. CFTC/NFA regulation — rare and strict
2. StoneX NASDAQ-listed parent — unmatched stability
3. 24+ year track record — proven through crises
4. Best US Forex Broker (Investopedia)
5. Multiple platforms — proprietary, MT4, MT5, TradingView
6. API trading — REST and FIX

Main weaknesses are limited instrument range (~80) and higher standard spreads than pure ECN brokers. The Commission account ($5/100K) offers competitive all-in costs.

---

*End of FOREX.com Deep-Dive Research Report*

# FOREX.com — Deep-Dive Broker Research

FOREX.com is one of the longest-established online forex brokers, operating since 1999. It was acquired by StoneX Group Inc. (formerly INTL FCStone), a Fortune 500 financial services firm ranked #59 by revenue ($64.38B in 2023). StoneX traces its roots to 1924 as a commodities broker.

## 6. FAQ

**Q1: Is FOREX.com available in the US?**
Yes. FOREX.com is one of the very few forex brokers regulated by the CFTC/NFA and available to US residents.

**Q2: What is the minimum deposit?**
$100 for all account types.

**Q3: Does FOREX.com support MetaTrader 5?**
Yes. MT5 was recently launched and is available in the US, Canada, and EU.

**Q4: Can I trade crypto on FOREX.com?**
Yes, via CFDs (outside the US) or actual crypto via Paxos (US clients).

**Q5: What is the RAW account?**
The RAW account offers spreads from 0.0 pips plus a commission of ~$5 per 100K traded. Best for active/high-volume traders.

**Q6: Does FOREX.com have an API?**
Yes. A REST API was recently launched for spread-only accounts, supporting algorithmic trading.

2. **Inactivity fee** — $15/month after 12 months without trading
3. **Limited social/copy trading** — no native copy trading features
4. **Stock/futures trading requires separate StoneX account** — not integrated
5. **RAW account all-in cost still ~1.14 pips** — trails best-in-class low-cost brokers
6. **US clients restricted** — no stock CFDs, limited leverage, no crypto CFDs

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

FOREX.com was founded in **2001** in **Parsippany, New Jersey, USA** by **Dr. Bill Dozer**. Originally known as **Gain Capital**, the company rebranded to FOREX.com to leverage its premium domain name recognition. The company is now a subsidiary of **StoneX Group Inc.** (NASDAQ: SNEX), the same Fortune 100 company that owns City Index.

FOREX.com is one of the largest and most well-known retail forex brokers in the **United States**, operating under CFTC/NFA regulation — one of the most stringent regulatory frameworks globally. The company also maintains a significant presence in the UK (FCA), Australia (ASIC), Canada, and other jurisdictions.

## EXPANDED REGULATION ANALYSIS

### CFTC/NFA (USA) — The Gold Standard

The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) and National Futures Association (NFA) represent the strictest regulatory framework for forex brokers globally:

- **Leverage:** Capped at **1:50** for retail (the lowest among major jurisdictions)
- **Capital Requirements:** Extremely high minimum capital requirements
- **Reporting:** Regular reporting to CFTC
- **Segregation:** Strict client fund segregation rules
- **NFA Membership:** Required for all US forex brokers
- **Investor Protection:** CFTC oversight, NFA arbitration

### FCA (UK)

Standard Tier-1 protections: segregated funds, FSCS compensation (up to £85,000), negative balance protection, leverage 1:30 retail.

### ASIC (Australia)

Standard Tier-1 protections: segregated funds, AFCA membership, leverage 1:30 retail.

### License Summary Table

| Regulator | Entity | Tier | Leverage | Key Feature |
|-----------|--------|------|----------|-------------|
| CFTC/NFA (US) | FOREX.com US | 1 | 1:50 | US market access (rare) |
| FCA (UK) | FOREX.com UK | 1 | 1:30 | £85K FSCS |
| ASIC (Australia) | FOREX.com AU | 1 | 1:30 | AFCA |
| IIROC (Canada) | FOREX.com Canada | 1 | 1:45 | Canadian regulation |
| FSA (Japan) | — | 1 | 1:25 | Japanese market |
| CIMA (Cayman) | — | 3 | 1:400 | Offshore, higher leverage |
| BVI FSC | — | 3 | 1:400 | Offshore |

## ADDITIONAL ANALYSIS

### FOREX.com for Specific Trader Types

**For US Traders:** FOREX.com is one of the few brokers legally offering forex trading to US residents under CFTC/NFA regulation. This regulatory requirement makes it the default choice for most US-based forex traders.

**For API Traders:** FOREX.com's REST and FIX API access makes it ideal for algorithmic traders who want programmatic market access.

**For Algorithmic Traders:** Full MT4/MT5 support with VPS hosting enables 24/7 EA operation.

**For Professional Traders:** The DMA account provides institutional-grade execution with full depth of market.

### Key Differentiator: US Market Access

The US retail forex market is one of the most difficult to serve due to the CFTC/NFA's extremely strict regulatory requirements:
- Leverage capped at 1:50
- High capital requirements for brokers
- Stringent reporting and compliance requirements

Only a handful of brokers are authorized to serve US retail forex clients, making FOREX.com's position highly defensible.
